title: Equity Research Analyst
description: Position Overview We are seeking an accomplished Equity Research Analyst to join our Portfolio Management Services (PMS) team. This role will be integral to building and managing the stock PMS portfolios for our clients. The role requires sector tracking and stock identification to enable portfolio decisions. The role also involves mentoring junior analysts and partnering closely with our Fund Manager, Research Committee, and Advisory team. If you are passionate about deep-dive research, connecting macro trends to stock performance, and influencing investment outcomes, this opportunity will allow you to make a direct and lasting impact. Key ResponsibilitiesEquity research Track assigned sectors across market caps, converting macro data/events into stock impact analysis Run stock screeners to identify trends and opportunities for PMS portfolios Track sectors allocated and come up with calls and monitor existing calls for averaging/exiting. Develop financial models, valuations (DCF, relative, comparable), and target prices Monitor existing stock calls and generate detailed research reports Evaluate off-coverage opportunities from screening models or market developments Contribute blog articles on stock reviews, sector updates, and market perspectives for customer engagement Maintain ethical standards in research and handling material non-public information Research team support Foster a knowledge-sharing environment through team discussions, debates, and continuous learning initiatives. Advisory team support Equip the advisory team with timely insights and sector updates to enhance their ability to engage with clients. Provide explanatory notes on significant stock/sector developments for client-facing communication. Conduct periodic training sessions for the advisory team to strengthen their market knowledge and client conversion skills. Support the advisory team withcustomer or potential customerinteractions answering queries or highlighting the benefits of PrimeInvestors PMS products Required QualificationsEducation & Experience Graduate or Post-graduate degree in Finance/Economics/Commerce OR a CFA charter holder 2-5 yearsof experience in equity research and issuing stock calls Skills & Competencies Ability to synthesize large amounts of data into actionable insights. Proficiency in using spreadsheets such as Excel or Google Sheets and familiarity with databases such as Ace Equity, Capitaline, etc. Strong grasp of macroeconomic and sectoral trends and their impact on equities. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present research clearly and persuasively. Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment. What We Offer Competitive compensation Opportunity to help design portfolios for high-net-worth clientele Exposure in building an innovative PMS product from the ground-up The chance to work with an industry-leading research team at the intersection of fintech and portfolio                    management. Career advancement opportunities within the organization About PrimeInvestor PrimeInvestor is an innovative B2C fintech company transforming the way individuals invest. We began by offering research-driven investment recommendations through a subscription-based model, delivered entirely online. Our flagship mutual fund portfolio analysis tool is a first-of-its-kind solution, setting a new benchmark in the industry. Building on this foundation, we are now expanding into Portfolio Management Services (PMS) for HNI clients. What sets us apart is our ability to create holistic, multi-asset portfolios customised through the intelligent use of technology to suit each clients unique financial goals. We are a passionate, high-performing team led by a founding group with decades of experience in fintech and investment research. At PrimeInvestor, youll be part of a dynamic and entrepreneurial environment where learning is constant and your contributions shape the future of wealth management.

title: Research Analyst
description: RESEARCH ANALYST / SENIOR RESEARCH ANALYST (LAW) BITS Law School, Mumbai invites applications for the position of Research Analyst / Senior Research Analyst to join the Centre for Research in Innovation Law for Shared Prosperity (CRISP). Location: BITS Law School, BITS Pilani Mumbai Campus Contract Period: 31 January 2026 to 31 December 2026 Job Description and Key Requirements: Undertake high-quality legal research culminating in law review articles in peer-reviewed journals and shorter research outputs on topics including, but not limited to, licensing of Standard Essential Patents (SEPs); evolving jurisprudence in SEP/FRAND litigation in India and other jurisdictions; the open standardisation system in the ICT industry; and legal, policy, and regulatory developments pertaining to the ICT and telecom sector in India, the European Union, the United Kingdom, and the United States. Lead the planning, coordination, and execution of a Law & Policy Fellowship Programme offered by BITS Law School for scholars and practitioners, including close coordination with internal and external stakeholders, curriculum design, and oversight of programme delivery. Liaise closely with the faculty associated with the Centre and with relevant departments of BITS Law School to ensure smooth execution of research and academic initiatives. Ensure the timely planning, coordination, execution, and submission of the Standard Essential Patents (SEP) Course, including adherence to internal timelines, preparation of academic and instructional materials, coordination with faculty members and external contributors, and overall quality control of course deliverables. Undertake any additional academic, research, coordination, or programme-related work that may arise during the term of engagement, provided such work pertains to the activities of the Centre for Technology Innovation & Law, including conferences, courses, fellowships, research projects, and related institutional initiatives. Offerings: A unique opportunity to work closely with and be mentored by the faculty of BITS Law School on cutting-edge issues such as SEP licensing, FRAND litigation, standard setting in telecom, and the interface between artificial intelligence, 6G, Internet of Things, and intellectual property law. Guidance and support for LL.M., Ph.D., and/or professional development plans. Opportunities to jointly publish research work, strengthen academic and professional profiles, and engage with leading experts in academia, industry, and policy circles in India and internationally. Integration into the intellectual and research community of BITS Law School and BITS Pilani. Required Qualifications: A five-year or three-year LL.B. degree from a recognised law school with strong academic performance. Demonstrable evidence of strong academic research and legal writing skills in relevant subject areas. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Preferred Attributes / Qualifications: LL.M. or Masters degree in Law, preferably with specialisation in technology law. Familiarity with topics such as SEP licensing, FRAND litigation, standardisation, telecom regulation, and technology law, demonstrated through coursework, research projects, academic publications, or dissertations. Two to five years of relevant work experience in legal academia, research roles, or law practice. Selected applicants are expected to join within a reasonable time period. A hybrid working arrangement may be considered for an initial period, if required and feasible.

title: IT Services Industry Analysts (Remote Working) - Fresher
description: Job purpose NelsonHall has ongoing vacancies for industry research analysts in IT services. These are remote working roles offering a high degree of flexibility to suit qualified candidates globally. These positions provide the opportunity to liaise with industry leaders and become a leading authority within your areas of expertise, advising leading IT services vendors on their offerings, strategic development and operational transformation journey, and providing market intelligence to major enterprises and multinationals. Duties and responsibilities Key responsibilities include: The production of detailed IT services market analyses The production of very detailed market analyses and profiles of IT Services providers Acting as a subject matter expert and demonstrating thought leadership to NelsonHall clients Market forecasting. Activities include: Interviewing senior personnel within major IT service providers Writing profiles and market analyses Assessing vendor capability and producing NEAT charts Attending industry analyst events Assisting vendor firms in their strategic development and operational transformation needs, and providing executive feedback on vendor positioning Tracking vendor news and announcements (including financials, M&A, contract wins, new offerings) Developing social media presence and propagating NelsonHall research and insights Updating the market forecast annually Assisting buy-side firms in identifying the art of the possible and suitable vendors. Please note this role involves analyzing the IT services market on behalf of IT services companies and their clients. It is not a business analyst role within IT or a solution architect role. Qualifications Keenness to learn and absorb relevant IT services domain knowledge is critical, as is a liking for MBA-style projects and intellectual curiosity. Plus, a desire to become one of the leading subject matter experts on the market for next generation approaches to IT services, including operational transformation. Would potentially suit individuals with: - Deep experience in the IT services industry, especially operational transformation, possibly in strategic marketing/product management roles and bid support - Experience in strategic sourcing of IT services for a major enterprise - MBAs with a desire to become an IT services domain expert and advise senior executives - Objective opinions and keen to be positioned as a thought leader in the market - Strong communication skills to facilitate building relationships across the market and managing discussions regarding vendor capability - Knowledge of the IT services market gained, for example, in a bid support role MBA qualifications are valued, but existing domain experience and enthusiasm are of greater importance. About NelsonHall NelsonHall is a global market analysis firm providing services to accelerate our clients' digital and operational transformation through the provision of research-led insight & thought leadership. Follow us on LinkedIn for latest news about our business, including new job opportunities. We have also just launched a LinkedIn Showcase page which you can 'Follow' via the link below: https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/nelsonhall-operations-transformation/

title: Data & Automation Analyst (AWS & Cloud Data)
description: Job Summary: Were looking for someone who is passionate about data engineering, automation and cloud platforms, with an interest in the music industry. Key Responsibilities: Design and automate data ingestion pipelines to process Excel and CSV files using Python (Pandas, NumPy). Store and manage structured and semi-structured data on AWS (Amazon S3, AWS RDS / Redshift). Assist in building and maintaining cloud-based data models and schemas optimized for analytics and reporting. Write and optimize SQL queries for data validation, aggregation and performance tracking. Support the development of ETL workflows using Python and AWS services (e.g., Lambda, Glue  exposure preferred). Skills & Requirements: Preferred B. Tech graduate. Minimum 1-2 years of experience in data analysis. Should be based out of Mumbai. Strong proficiency in Excel/CSV data handling, with hands-on Python experience for data automation and ETL. Working knowledge of SQL, relational databases and core data architecture concepts. Familiarity with AWS data services (S3, RDS/Redshift, Lambda/Glue exposure is a plus). Basic knowledge of cloud data flows, JavaScript and Git/version control are desirable additions.

exposure to Salesforce is a plus Strong interest in systems, business operations, and technology Proficiency in Excel/ Google Sheets; ability to work with large datasets Excellent technical writing, analytical, and problem-solving skills Experience or internship in private equity, consulting, or sales or customer lifecycle environment Understanding of one or more business process (Sales, Customer Success, Revenue Operations) that lead into accounting functions Active Salesforce certifications (e.g., Salesforce Certified Administrator, Platform App Builder, Sales/ Service/ Community Cloud Consultant) Exposure to Salesforce configuration (reports, dashboards, workflows) and basic understanding of integrations or data flows (Suite Script/JavaScript) Familiarity with customer lifecycle reporting concepts and how CRM data supports downstream billing or financial processes Experience supporting multi-entity or multi-business environments
